item.title: Hello world!
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
This is my very first post.
Its like batting first in the game of cricket. you do not know where you're headed. but what you do know is where you have been. There are a billion things my inquisitive mind ponders upon in the endless 24 hours of a day and here I am to pen down just a few of them.
feel free to interact and provide valuable feedback and criticism as to providing me a clearer picture of the images my brain so often creates.
Looking forward to rains.
The waterman

item.title: The good, The bad and The "you"
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
To think of duality in nature, to be straight about it.. To think of good and evil is I presume just something we make up in our own brains. Somehow whatever you are now sitting there reading this is a result of your upbringing your childhood and your past.(how? Google “Freud”) and nobody can change that. Not even you. Thus the fact of the matter remains the same. All you need to do is accept yourself the way you are and then think of people accepting you. Coming back to chalking out the good from the bad. Pretty simple huh? I really don’t think so let’s remember the “Wise-man” Archimedes. The bloke said when you put something in water the amount of water spilled is equal to the mass of the thing you put in that water. Similarly Newton! Let me bring up about another funny story… I mean man is such an instantaneous being( we shall definitely talk about this one). Tonight you might dream of something tomorrow something else. Now that depends on your day. But Newton? That guy thought of gravity when a Heroic apple decided to fall on his numb head. Why numb? Well come on! He used the loo almost every day I presume? And all that empty head time we all spend in the loo thinking of the lamest ideas ever wouldn’t he ever wonder why his scientifically marvelous pooper never went up. How long will you wait for that apple to come and fall on your head? Let’s talk today on the duality. The British Empire considered Mister Bhagat Singh a terrorist but the later was a martyr for the Indian nation and is still revered for his heroic martyrdom. Perspective is the word I feel important and deserves pondering upon. Because perspective is the only thing you have when you have motive. Motive comes from our will. Will comes from perspective again. It’s a vicious circle of its own kind! And it’s a marvelous thing. How? Well as some old guy said the need is the mother of all inventions. Now needs are based on perspectives. Now “how” you look at things is important. I will not bore you with the omnipresent half glass of water and how we dissect our species into optimists and pessimists because that’s not really our point here. Perspective is nothing but our way of looking at things. If we believe strongly enough we have a notion of faith and we end up believing in that particular thing. Good bad? That depends on our need! The same human who is capable of a loving and caring emotion is also capable of hating or being envious at someone. What sets us apart from other developed life forms is this very aspect of life … perspective … the one that differs from each and every one of us. I like the “Eagles” (not the bird) but you might be a hard-core “linkin park” fan, does that make you a good guy or a bad one? Well I would say good guy looking from your buddies point of view and bad from your grandmas. Because at her age she likes Kenny G and probably at her age you will too because that is exactly how our brains react to aging and that is again a very important factor on building a perspective. The duality that we were earlier focusing upon is nothing but our standalone view of something we look at. Imagine a guy...or a girl if you’re a feminist. (And/ or if you think everyone should be). So imagine this person (now that’s more subtle) is capable of living on…. Yes as simple as that. Imagine somehow this person has been capable to dodge death itself. (How? Remember you are imagining this)(for hard-core science geeks let’s all presume that the body is somehow able to detoxify itself of all toxins that we create and hence stop aging) coming back to this person. A person who has dodged death itself and is hence living on & on & on. He/she wont naturally become the grandfather of all knowledge just because they lived for a gazillion years because it’s after all one person at one place at one particular time. So if you asked this elder who had lived for say a 10,000 years about some definite historical event he shouldn’t be liable to answer that! Hence again it will be his/ her perspective on concluding whatever reports they have of the world they have seen. now consider any normal person has a motive , imagine any motive , lean back and just let your imagination flow… his motive can be knowledge, happiness, anger, truth, revenge etc. ., etc., every single motive shall lead him to a different path and hence a different destination. Now weather that destination makes him content or not is another long subject but it’s the motive that made him perceive something and led him there.

So how do we categorize good and evil finally? To be honest there is no such thing. Both of these phenomenal qualities reside inside us.

I Quote John Milton: 'The mind is its own place, and in itself can make a heaven of hell, a hell of heaven The Waterman.

item.title: Delay
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
Thought about the poem: The poem is an attempt to describe the feelings of a cancer patient...a kid suffering from Osteosarcoma.
Delay
A thought occurred to me That I couldn’t bend my knee Had a pain in my muscle Yet ignored it completely
Kept getting the “medicine” I knew it never would help me And the pain now grew beyond my knee Keeping me in utmost agony
Day after day the pain grew Soon I realized the days left were few Why didn’t I report the day I saw The limp in my walk, the fallen jaw
I don’t wanna take pain along I’m too small to sing this song But, I would say this before parting Just say it, don’t keep waiting..
-- Mukul Godbole

item.title: Mind games
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
How can we categorize something into tangible and intangible? To be very vague, something that we can touch and feel is obviously tangible and the opposite intangible. But what about the future? Take a little time out and pause everything else and just think about it. What are we? If not for the time we live in. The other day I saw an insurance company advertisement on the television. Not to sound skeptic about the concept. But somewhere people nowadays are very easily convinced that they are definitely going to die(every human knows that but fails to realize) and after their death, their family needs the most important thing than the life of the loved one, “money”, and the person being insured can then die in peace when Mr “hooded guy with the scathe” comes knocking at their doorstep. People dedicate their lives to mundane jobs and work lives that they forget that they are an inevitable part of that same family, who somewhere wants them to b together and spend quality time. Instead people choose to work harder and harder to earn money and also pay premiums. I am overwhelmed at this fact as to how can one decide willfully to pay up because they want their families to have a better future after they’re gone leaving their families with easy money? And a Better future…did I say better future? Look back. Is the future tangible? To make death an USP or a unique selling proposition is a very powerful asset. Like no other. Every product needs an USP without which it fails to create the need of the later.
A million thanks to mass media and marketing strategies. Man has discovered a way to burrow in a way into the human brain, to create a demand where it is not present in the first place. I’m not here to plagiarize and stick a heel into the marketing sector. All I am trying to say is they have excelled at manipulative skills. And what do they manipulate? Everyone. The age old marketing strategies have become so synonymous that we have assumed those concepts as common sense. For example why do you think the color blue and pink represent boys and girls respectively? It was only a marketing stunt pulled off very effectively in the 19th century in Europe. And it kind of stuck until modern times. Who says flowers are feminine? Ask yourself. Anyways moving on. To promote ones product or service its but obvious that the void of requirement has to be created. Many a time’s conspiracy theorists believe that the outbreaks of deadly epidemics are well orchestrated by the makers so that a particular brand of vaccines or antibiotics is sold in vast numbers and vast profits. (let us all assume that’s fiction and feel safe in the homes we all live in… momentarily) so tangibility is under questioning when we say that almost all our voids ( or requirements ) are man-made. The key point to note in this tricky sentence above is very simple. If one spends more on experience rather than that on products and services one can gain more value. There is a reason for that statement. Everything we buy today is mainly consumables. And these consumables are rated on basis of quality (?). Let’s define quality. Something that proves that it meets or sets high standards than or for other products (in laymen terms) but in comparison to what? In order to prove one particular product someone else has to take a fall. For instance competition and that is exactly why marketing thrives on this competition.
Let us all take an imaginary stroll through a bustling market in a city of your choice. Let this market be filled of local vendors. The ones you can haggle over for the price of goods. Say clothes. Now you buy something you like at a fair price you bargained and return home happily. Now let us go up an escalator of a shopping mall. Obviously the ambiance and the neatly ordered ironed clothes categorized and highlighted on 7 feet tall mannequins look enchanting. But the fabric is the so called “Quality” fabric and the maker is a big brand. Both are clothes, same fashion, same material, different maker. You choose according to your spending power. An average Indian shopper will prefer the market and a brand conscious shopper will never look beyond the mall. So now Tangibility is a choice. It suddenly becomes flexibility. A choice between necessity and luxury. Funniest part is I also saw something called a luxury soap advertisement the other day. How can that soap be any different from normal soaps? How can a deluxe version of something be better? I went on to compare the contents of the soap which were exactly the same. But still, to create that brand value you have to make that tangible product hard to get. It’s like setting high standards for a kid. Saying look son, anyone can buy a Maruti but for a Porsche you need to be bigger than you think. Now it depends on the kid if he grows up into being a person who prioritizes necessities or someone who is lavishly fond of luxuries. And as long as prominence is given to luxuries in a glorified brand manner the society will always have the rich and the poor.
The waterman

item.title: It's a Bad Bad world out there!!
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded: Psychology is a very important aspect when you need to understand your customers. Lets take a sneak peek as how you can implement psychology to your own marketing campaign's advantage: 1. Run Emotional Ideas Studies have shown emotional and psychological appeals resonate more with consumers than feature and function appeals. In advertising, benefits, which often have a psychological component generally, outsell features. Demonstrating how a new computer will improve a potential customer’s life tends to have more influence rather than explaining how it works. Salespeople have long understood the power of emotional appeals. In the 18th century, when the contents of a Brewery were being auctioned off, the auctioneer said: "We are not here to sell boilers and drums, but the potentiality of growing rich beyond the dreams of greed." 2. Highlight Your Flaws It’s no secret that consumers tend to doubt marketing claims for good reasons. Many simply aren’t credible. One way to raise credibility is to point out your product’s shortcomings. Among the most famous examples was an ad for Volkswagen, which contained a one-word headline: "Lemon." Opening body copy below a VW photo read: "This Volkswagen missed the boat. The chrome strip on the glove compartment is blemished and must be replaced. Chances are you wouldn’t have noticed it; Inspector Kurt Kroner did." The ad went on to discuss a "preoccupation with detail." The Lemon ad became a textbook example of how to optimize credibility. 3. Reposition Your Competition In the book ‘Positioning: The Battle for Your Mind’, the author probes into the limited slots consumers have in their brain for products and services, and the importance of positioning one’s business in the ideal slot. They also write about repositioning i.e. changing the position a business occupies in consumers’ minds. A prominent example of repositioning the competition is when the Jif brand launched the "Choosy moms choose Jif" campaign; competitors were suddenly repositioned as products for mothers who didn’t give a damn about the food their kids consumed. What mother didn’t want to think of herself as a choosy mom? 4. Promote Exclusivity Near the top of Maslow’s hierarchy pyramid sits self-esteem. People want to feel important; like they’re part of an exclusive group. That’s why advertising copy sometimes says: "We’re not for everyone." The other day i came across the 'Jaguar XF' ad with Kareena Kapoor pitching exclusively this very aspect of the car. They branded themselves into a car owned by a select few. Typical skimming. Perhaps the most famous modern example of exclusivity in advertising is the American Express tagline: "Membership has its privileges." But to make an exclusivity appeal work in the long run, marketers must mean what they say. Empty claims tend to be counterproductive. 5. Introduce Fear, Uncertainty, and Doubt Fear, uncertainty, and doubt, or FUD, is often used legitimately by businesses and organizations to make consumers stop, think, and change their behavior. FUD is so powerful that it’s capable of nuking the competition into dust. You can browse to the fear appeal article on Wikipedia to help you further. lets take a very recent example of those anti-smoking ads we so often see on daily mass media. Between 1980 and 2012, smoking among Indian men decreased from 33.8 per cent to 23 per cent. To understand your customers you need to understand your business and what value propositions you set forth. With a clear picture one can successfully convert leads into clientele. The waterman.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: It is definitely a bad bad world out but as bad things are contagious good things are not. Karna in Mahabharata also knew that things which he has to do are bad but due to gratitude he still continued. to revert a help from bad people people help in bad deed.
Very true ... here I speak about how we can make someone believe they are doing the right thing. Watch out for more interesting topics. And feel free to post!
Thanks for the read! Above is just a gist of what goes on in general. Very few can look beyond the lucrative world that has come to be. Thanks again and keep reading !

World is indeed bad but not everyone n everything, every individual have a different view for things done. All the experiments done may not give appropriate result but that doesn't stop one to continue, but yes I wish whatever is done by companies or organisation should not play a villain character, where they just make people think what they want. Well that will never happen. Good job waterman.

item.title: draft: pandemic, gods weakened
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
“We were lucky there wasn’t much traffic today, would’ve been a pain in the butt if we had to go late inspite of leaving on time, plus all the glares and all the passing comments ughh, even last sunday, remember how the Erikdottirs were whispering? I literally felt like I should have turned around and said…”

Matteo was not paying attention to his mothers rants as he blankly stared out of the back seat of the old VW Golf. They were headed to church for the funeral service one of the neighbors who had mysteriously lost his life while watering the plants in his backyard. No one could say what happened. On checking the security cameras around the house they could only see him choke on something before falling over the shrubs. Asphyxiation maybe, who knows, who cares, if Covid-19 isn’t killing you something else will. Matteo remembered how the other day Zuzka was reading an article which said how more than 1.5 million people die due to diarrhea each year worldwide. “Well fragile humans cant even fucking take a shit without dying” he thought and chuckled.

“Whats so funny?” Asked Mabel who was otherwise busy typing away on her phone on the other side of the seat. “someone’s dead and he’s smiling to himself mom” she complained to the mother.



People were only allowed in groups of 20 for social gatherings in highly regulated and distanced environments.

item.title: Scribble Splash!
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
Who we are

At Scribble Splash, we’re not just about writing; we’re about creating ripples in the vast ocean of life. Our founder, a passionate writer, thinker, and life coach, has dedicated their life to helping others find their voice, craft their story, and navigate the complex journey of life with grace and resilience.

Our vision

We stand on the pillars of creativity, clarity, and transformation. Our content is crafted to enlighten, engage, and encourage our readers to explore the depths of their being and the heights of their potential.

“'Exceptional and stimulating articles that inspire the reader to engage actively'.”

— A Happy Reader

Join the Splash!

What We Offer

  • Thought-Provoking Articles: Dive into a sea of articles that challenge your perceptions and offer new perspectives on life, creativity, and personal growth.
  • Life Coaching Sessions: Personalized sessions designed to help you overcome obstacles, achieve your goals, and live a life aligned with your true purpose.
  • Creative Workshops: Join our interactive workshops to unleash your creativity, improve your writing skills, and connect with like-minded individuals.
  • Inspirational Blog Posts: Regular blog updates that provide a dose of motivation, practical tips for everyday living, and stories of transformation.

Are you ready to make a splash in your life? Subscribe to our newsletter for the latest updates and follow us on our journey to make the world a more thoughtful and vibrant place.

Let’s create, inspire, and grow together at Scribble Splash!

Let's make something together!

item.title: monkey see Monkey do
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded: In a previous article we slightly touched the topic on how the mass media is responsible for wiping our brains clean and filling in tailored thoughts. Let’s peek into the immense power of suggestion. We as of today live in a very intricately orchestrated world where the few in power control the millions. Is it possible to even make someone do your bidding? Is it possible to make people obey? Is it possible to make people follow? The answer to all the questions is a simple “yes”. Next time they’re showing chimps on natgeo don’t switch the channels, as the proof of that “yes” above lies right there. How? Just observe their behaviour. Man is not much different from the apes. Except we think higher of ourselves. But the similarities and social behaviors are not much different. Ever heard of the phrase “ monkey see, monkey do”? it applies more to people than to apes. Now some of us think of ourselves as indomitable, solid, and of sound minds and actually believe that we do what we wish and not what we are told to do. Ask yourself. While choosing the career you think you chose yourself, who all did you consult? How many of your friends, relatives, or peers had chosen the same careers? still think you chose it yourself? Okay. Let’s think of something else. I assume you are a someone who has done shopping online. Now from the vast list of items you compare the most similar ones, probably for an indian buyer your first go is at the price. Secondly you check the reviews. What if your next smartphone hunt has brought you across two good products product ‘A’ has relatively good reviews than the product ‘B’. What will you choose? You might argue that how will you come to know which product to trust? Hence you chose ‘A’? Trust. Isn’t that something that makes us follow? Makes us obey? Throw a small baby in the air and it giggles for it knows you’re going to catch it on its way down. Hence it becomes play. What if you sat on a rollercoaster, they tie your safety gear and tuck you in safely and you’re told it’s bound to fail on its way down? Scary? From time and again we hear the phrase do as your told. Frequently so while we were kids. To inculcate reflex perception about a particular thing you have to build up a routine. For example say you have a habit of keeping your watch on the dresser every evening. And one day you purposely keep it somewhere else. The next morning you will definitely absent-mindedly reach out for the watch on the dresser. Now that is simply a habit. Now once you trust someone it's just like another habit. The only difference is we trust people and we get habituated to things. Or in some cases vise versa. Lets come back to monkey see monkey do, we tend to choose something that is suggested readily than we choose something out of the box. Here’s a small game for you to try out. In India its very simple to convince people. So you know what I say is true to the word. all you need to do is while walking on a street join hands and bow down at a certain stone. Well only requirements are that the stone must be visible and all peculiarities are an obvious pro. After a month or so you shall see devotees lining up to offer their prayers at the stone for you have initiated a chain reaction. If you see a grown man dressed sharp in the street and he is asking for free food, you will only give him a cold shoulder but if the same man looks needy and lost you might actually give that guy a sandwich. Coming back to effective decision-making and being a badass who does what he wants we really aren't who we think we are. Not all of our decisions are made according to our own choices but only because of external entities and the power of suggestion. Take a look back at where you are now. And the decisions made hitherto are not entirely our own. So ultimately the power of commanding someone to obey is not that a difficult task. Gone are the days when you have to force someone to do something against their will. The population has become so dependant on suggestions that people don't feel the need to apply their own brains to ponder over a topic and arrive to a result. Rather they take ready-made decisions on the basis of suggestions. Nowadays instead of implementing something, all you have to do is say a few things in favor of a particular thing and people will blindly follow your lead for that is how our brains are hard-wired. suggestion is a great tool for businesses. For each and every product or service it plays an extremely important role in bringing revenue. suggestion is not just saying hey go try our this new shampoo its great! suggestion plays a role in systematic brain washing an individual with repetition. Now that is where the mass media comes into play. when I say repetition, i want you to visualise your favorite ad. for someone of my generation the jingles on radio are a nostalgic reminders of how technologically paralysed was advertising before the scientific advances in the field of mass media. Television came on stage and not long before it changed its own name to an idiot box. people got addicted to it and even today one cannot live without a simple T.V. for more than a week. our minds have become prone to take in useless knowledge. hence we never separate the inputs we get into valuable and invaluable. This is when the mind stops working and primeval instincts come to play. when the mind is used to not refining useful data it pushes the information to another part of the brain where it deduces the subject on basis of existing facts rather than logic. So next time you see an apple customer turned sales guy talking about the pros of his phone you'll know what to do. Politicians are another great example of how effectively this theory works. Each and every citizen knows that every politician is corrupt to a certain level and its a well-known saying to not to trust a senators smile. but on the contrary when that same guy makes motivational speeches and promises to be the provider of all those necessities which were absent before. yet you elect one of the goons who made an impact. now it solely depends on his/her branding skills. the one who was able to market himself will definitely win the elections. and the other will forfeit the deposit. hence you on the other hand did as you were told. At this juncture if your told that you were a believer of Y2K and 2012. I would bet surely as even you had a doubt in mind in the year 1999 that somehow the world will stop spinning and in 2012 extensive coverage given by the news channels ( who apparently have nothings these days and make news out of soap opera story lines) made you think in 2012 some solar flare might hit your bottom and make a bratwurst out of you! today all we can do is but laugh at our gullible nature. The waterman.

item.title: End of an era
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded: I was listening to "smells like teen spirit" by nirvana 1991, closed my eyes and saw Kurt Cobain mouthing " hello, hello, hello, how low!" and thats when i went back. Back to a time when people were oblivious to these blogs that you are reading right now. Nor did they worry about their persona they created on social networks. I went to a time when kids actually came out of their houses and played in the grounds. A time I myself cherish so much more than that I do now. The 1990s marked the end not only of a century but also a millennium. The decade leading up to the year 2000 saw a lot of change and excitement, with many important events that shaped not only the 1990s but our lives since then. That change happened in every way you can imagine – politically, technologically, and culturally. With the Cold War over, Nelson Mandela free, and the Internet changing the way we work and live, the events of the 1990s provided the perfect bridge between the outrageous 80s and the dawn of the new century. I remember the quickest way to convey a message was to make a trunk booking and the operator called you back with the connected call. Text messaging was yet to arrive and only text messages I ever got were written on a crumpled piece of paper. I remember attending a certain lecture by some famous person as I was a kid I don't really remember his name but he was there to deliver a speech on upcoming careers. This happened in circa 1997. I remember him talking about how email would replace snail-mail and people wondered why he mocked the postal department so much. I myself was fascinated as to how can I push some buttons and the alphabets would then appear on the screen (!) and was completely blown away when the computer class teacher showed us "Dave" the MS-DOS game... it was simply magic! Shortly then I remember stacking up those video game cassette chips that we shoved in the Nintendo consoles that were wired into the T.V's only to turn into super Mario and fight against a tortoise- dragon like figure to save the princess (who apparently was always in a different castle) Inverters were a long-shot so power cuts meant playing dumb-charades and antakshari! Our kids might never understand why we say "Stay tuned" or 'tune into" a specific channel. For touchscreen era is way different than radios and a time where FM was something where you could listen to episodic stories and could call the stations to make them play your song that you could listen to on your Walkman. Sundays usually start up late these days. Waking up to "ooooh yeah last night was a killer." Probably a migraine or a hangover or just a faint reminder of the long week at work in front of a computer screen and Sunday is moreover sleep more and order a pizza day. Now look back and you see being exited to wake up early to watch weekly soaps like F.R.I.E.N.D.S, or for the doodarshan folks it was mahabharat and ramayan and who can forget the Simpsons? They are still there and doing a helluva job! and the youth was swept away by "just mohobbat" on Sony. Even Kids had their share of fun in the 90's for Hannah Barbara studios set up an arsenal of shows to keep kids glued to the cubical T.V's all day long! The Flintstones and scooby-doo to name a few. Movies like Pretty Woman, American Pie and American Beauty were the only modes of getting on some naughty fun alone time. The other day I was traveling in the train and happened to see a guy with headphones shoved in to his ipod. How oblivious was he to the boom boxes people carried on their shoulders and elders looked at them with hateful eyes for being noisy. Now it just cracks me up. Forget about CD-Roms I still remember taking a pencil to rewind those damned things called cassettes and they would get all sticky in the monsoons due to humidity, well lets not get carried away! To meet up with someone actually meant something and its was so much different with skyping them. Fast food restaurants and cafes with cooler themes became second-home for youngsters. And for the youngsters came MTV. MTV flooded with new artists. Songs that were loved were “O Sanam -Luckly Ali,” “Tanha dil -Shaan,” “made in India-Alisha” and “pari hoon main- suneeta rao to name a few. Cheap posters and postcards of Actors, actresses and Music Sensations were pandemic in stores, those days. Michael Jackson, Madonna, Backstreet Boyz and Venga Boys ruled this particular decade. Adding rap in a song became prevalent during 90s’. MTV gave birth to new trend and lifestyle among youngsters, mostly school and college kids. Archies and Hallmark Cards back then were selling like a hot piece of cake. The celebrations of Valentines Day became dramatic. Expression of love, buying gifts for the loved one and party culture ended up becoming viral. People were active back then. I am not all saying that the masses have become lethargic now but all I mean to say is that the modes have changed and so has the mindset and this is exactly what makes the 90's unique for it was a decade of change. A decade that saw these transitions happen. A decade that saw people change their lifestyles with technology that was slowly but steadily taking them to where we are now. Things were pretty much different back then. Some of us were busy creating auto books for friends to sign and write. Some of us might still have them and they look so real than the actual facebook profiles. Signing off an Auto-book or known to some as a slam book was one way of keeping personal records of friends in schools and colleges back then. It was pandemic. Everyone in the class used to own an auto-book and every other friend used to write something on it. Undoubtedly, everyone used to write about mundane things they loved most; Films, actors, singers, albums with personal picture intact in every profile. Archies’ cards, VCRs, Radio FMs and Cassettes were religious aspects of daily life. I saw people all excited about the Hubble. Back in the day people gave Kodak smiles for photos and to xerox something meant to make copies. I never really figured out why people kept saying that whatever happened to princess Diana was so not an accident. Video Shops in the city were crowded for people wanting to rent the latest movie VCD to see on their brand new VCR's. Lovable British buffoon Rowan Atkinson began his first run as the bumbling Mr. Bean, who combined physical slapstick humor with absurdly hilarious situations. Kids watched the original Ninja Turtles & Power Rangers . . . The coolest way to say hello was Michaelangelo's Cowabunga Dude! and joey's How you doin! and Macarena was the one song that made you tap your feet. You remember before we realized all this would eventually disappear and so will some of the things we see and use now. well.... who knows ? *Sigh* Who would have thought you'd miss the 90's so much!!!! The Waterman.

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: Well written! Got a nostalgic feeling! :-)
Thank you .. Share your most memorable part of the 90's
very true what u written.... 90's...really a enjoyable environment for us as compared to these kids... bt change is the law of nature and for survival we have to accept...

item.title: To forget is to kill
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded: Every civilization has its own way of communicating through its generations. Monuments, sculptures, idols etc. are only the creations we can see and touch. But more than that they leave behind a language. A language is something that connects each and every one of us to what we are and what we were, for communication is the root cause of all the developments our species has been able to make. But recently I came across something very disturbing. There are 6,000 languages spoken by humans alive today. According to UNESCO estimates, half of them will go extinct in the next 50 years! 3000 languages, gone. By the time I grow old and the sands of time are about to run empty, 50% of the languages on this planet will die too, says BBC. And unlike plants or animals, which can be conserved and bred, language can only survive if people keep speaking it. Google however has come up with a language conservation program but then again it will only be an archive of literature, for native speakers have either died out or moved on to another mode of communication. Somehow it makes me curious. Our violent history of mankind is only but an expansionist theory and somewhere man has always tried to implicate his ways and customs on the weaker counterpart. And the language is but the heart and soul of every civilization, take that out and after a few decades only tourists will visit the places and click selfies in front of the dilapidated monuments that are left behind. Take European expansionists for instance, the British, the Spaniards, the French they all went to sea to trade and explore but they explicitly remembered to do one thing without fail, they expanded their culture by teaching their language to those they acquired. Today French is taught in schools here in India but some third world African countries have citizens that are already fluent in French. After the second world war Germany was divided into the DDR and BRD and the east was under the U.S. of A and the west under the U.S.S.R and today one can clearly see the influence of English and Russian on the native language. This is exactly what happens to the language when the masses move on. The once purely Germanic language today is a mixture of words from all over. Youth today prefer saying " sorry " instead of "Es tut mir Leid", but it is fortunate. For there are many other languages that are suffering a fate that is much worse. Languages like Amurdak of Australia, Apiaka of Brazil and Ainu of japan have just one native speaker left. and when they forget a word, its gone forever. That is the slow process of how a language comes to extinction. We all studied biology in some point of our lives and have accidentally come across the Latin names of certain entities, Latin, very old language, gone, ever given a thought that as of this day Latin is not spoken on the streets of Rome anymore, where at one point on the grounds of which an entire empire was built. Nature is like water. It tries to calm down as soon as the disturbance settles down. Similarly when a world was without communication and networking people who lived in small groups developed their own language and in wider areas they developed different dialects of the same. And with globalization comes this need for simplicity of communication. We have seen the euro, the one currency used by a number of nations, god knows when but amero is also something that is being thought of. If currencies can become common, then the day is not far that the forth coming generations shall see one global currency and one global language. ( if they don't go to war and kill each other on the way!) Lets look at it the other way round. Some groups of the human population are pretty tight on sticking to their own language take China, Russia and North Korea or the Indian state of Tamil Nadu for Instance, they on a certain level despise other languages and are proud rednecks of their own. Not to say that this is wrong. But every coin has to faces. Pros of such attitude are many. The language lives and so does each and every aspect of the culture, The masses are acclimatized to thinking in one language so they respond quickly (how?) Here's how, if I ask "What time is it?" to an Indian his brain first decodes this simple question into his native language, then he forms a sentence, does a grammar check and then spits the answer back. Result? Look for yourself. Coming back, Native speakers are hence on a upper-hand in that case but there is also a flip side. Communication barriers are lurking behind every wall. They have to rely on interpreters and apps for communicating with people outside their ethnic group and hence the concept of being bilingual or multilingual is on a high tide in these days. We are a generation that has not only begun to globalize but also a generation that survives on a tightly knit global network. But More than 50% of the world's languages are located in just eight countries: India, Brazil, Mexico, Australia, Indonesia, Nigeria, Papua New Guinea, and Cameroon. In these countries and around them are the areas that are the most linguistically diverse in the world. Ever been to Dubai? That middle eastern part of the world that doesn't really feel middle eastern? No matter where you're from if your English is good you can get along really well. The main reason languages die out is because native speakers are using it, and they are not being transmitted to their children. They prefer that their children learn the one global language everyone is familiar with. It is upto the people to choose to be multilingual as then and only then they can preserve a language. The best example here is of the preservationists who are fighting back in some places. In New Zealand, for example, the Maori have opened nursery schools where elders conduct classes, all in Maori. They call them “language nests.” In Mexico, after refusing to talk to each other for years, the last two surviving speakers of Ayapaneco, a thousand-year-old south American language, recently reunited to try and save their language(do read the article by Chris Taylor on mashable). And several organizations like the "Living Tongues Institute for Endangered Languages" or National Geographic (click link) are going to great lengths to document and record languages at the brink of disappearance. Even Google is putting its resources at the service of safeguarding linguistic diversity through its Endangered Languages Project (click link). Is the loss of language just "ethno-linguistic natural selection" or Neocolonialism? It is clear that with the death of a language, there is the loss of a great accepted culture that has once been there before. "Only a few cultures erected grandiose architectural monuments by which we can remember their achievements. But all cultures encode their genius in their languages, stories and lexicons," says K. David Harrison, author of 'When Languages Die'. It is but every persons responsibility to make sure that they pass on their heritage to the forthcoming generations for that is the only way the new ones will still stay connected to its roots. so as the maori saying goes, Kite wawe ia koe! The waterman
item.excerpt:encoded: Why should we care if a language goes poof? For with the death of a language withers its rich tradition.

item.title: The "On/Off" Switch of life
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
How can we figure out what is it that we want from our lives? How can we expect the world to behave in a way we see fit?
the answer is exactly what you think it is. If you are expecting a straight forward solution to the dilemma above, well sadly there is none. But on the other hand isn't it a good thing to not have any ready-made, add hot water and serve recipe solutions in life? If all came to him automatically, if without any efforts he got everything he wished, man would then forget one very important thing in that case, he would definitely loose passion. Because it is for the want of something we pursue our dreams, look up to that maslows hierarchy of needs, and you can see all your demands fall into those stupid categories. Its not the achievements that matter most but its the journey that brings out the fun. Everyone wants to be a pilot or an actor in their childhood, but very few stick to it and make it happen. Others on the other hand keep going on and decide what they want as a result of what is actually needed.
There are two kinds of people who look to these things in two entirely different ways. Firstly, we have the believers, the ones who focus on what they do and what they wanna achieve and make sure they get there. and secondly there are the ones who move on! The reasons for these migration of aims and needs can be many, there might be economical reasons, family problems, time management etc.etc. But all these factors build up and shove people off from their destined path in life and hence they take up easier and the more obvious pathways to earn.
If we knew our future would we still keep doing what we are doing now? wouldn't every decision we made, change our future? Therefore our future is what we make of it.
To all of those who think that they have lost hope into pursuing their dreams in whichever field, then it might be a specific art, a dream job, a dream career whatever you can or have thought of, I would therefore say don't let go! There might be opportunities that might still be lurking around the corner and just before you are going to get them you loose hope and give up.
Its like planting a seed, how do you go about it? You toss a bean in the ground, sprinkle water, wriggle your fingers at it and out pops a beanstalk! wow wouldn't that be something huh? Farmers would be superheroes in that case, but no, most of us stop watering the seed just before the germinated leaflets are about to hit the surface from below the ground. To make sure that tree grows you gotta be patient. but to expect patience in every case is idiotic, Imagine you're in a hospital, granny's not well, doctor says we need to operate her and you've gotta deposit a certain amount and you tell the doc; "Man listen I've got this really great opportunity coming right up and when it does I'm gonna be a millionaire and then I can pay you!" The sick granny will come out and kick you in the shin! Jokes apart.
Look at almost every father and in his eyes you can definitely see that he had to let go of his own personal dreams to make sure he went into a monotonous job that provided steady money for the family. This is a world wide phenomenon, and one should not feel lost if he has to sacrifice his personal aspirations to purposely lie to himself and choose something that is good for his stable future. Money! the whole point of todays gibberish, moolah! I hear parents quite often telling their children, "Look at some so and so's son he's taken up computers and now is working in a big banner M.N.C. and you? you are here painting meadows! Ughh!" I can only laugh at such people, just for fun, imagine a world where every single person was either a lawyer or a doctor or a software nerd on the line giving tech support? who would then be a firefighter? Not once have I heard someone say, son! you are good at making idols go be a sculptor. Kids nowadays have moved of to being couch potatoes. Its the summer holidays that freak me out. If playgrounds were alive they would go complaining how ignored they feel, times have changed. there was a time when a dog was man best friend, these days its an android. This eventually leads to a big lifestyle change when these "couch-potatoes" grow up. To a guy that loves to sit, a job to sit in font of a computer screen will ever be so lucrative, they grow into trees that can give away anything and everything for their social status, the guys end up being abusive husbands and the girls grow into career oriented slapsticks that toss their kids to a babysitter. (prove me wrong here) all the frustration of their corporate lives comes out when the working parents come back home. You might wonder what has that got to do with all of this? I say everything. The youth today is bedazzled with the lifestyle the "employees" get to live. The way they go shopping and partying every weekend, they way these "suited corporate kuttas" swipe cards at every drop of a hat. What they fail to see is the debts these employees have and the impending EMI's of their credit cards. The corporate world has taken the generation in to a vicious circle. You earn, you spend, you spend more than you earn, you get debts, you get debts but you can't quit, for you have put up a social image of yourself that you cant afford to tarnish! Plus you might loose an opportunity to go abroad "courtesy of your company", in days to come!! Boy you've gotta let go! Do what pleases you no one cares about how much you spend and show off.
Because even if you don't no one will ever notice.
To wrap up; Every career is as marvelous to pursue. Not one bit of knowledge ever goes for waste. At any point in life if you feel, I am not happy or satisfied with whatever it is that I do? Stop! and walk out! Your dreams will never let you down. And most importantly, If ever you feel all hope is lost, look around, there's always a way out of every problem.
The waterman

item.title: Meeting a Beer and Sausage!
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
Not long ago, I met a certain German delegate who was on a business tour, and I was given a team that had to brief him and his colleague about the current ongoings in the company, things that he was concerned about, Back then I held a position wherein I had make sure the meeting went on without any glitches. the first thing I Had to keep in mind was that that guy was a German, a race of people that is drop dead punctual, even if they aren't the peculiar stereotypes back home, Germans do behave as living time pieces when they travel on business. Secondly Germans hate to linger in long conversations. This time particularly, they both had a beer in their hand, and said only one word, "Los". which means "fire away". One has to make sure that coming to the point is something the whole conversation should be all about. Bullet points, pros, cons, a brief comparative analysis and practical solutions that can save time and increase productivity. Thats all. It took only 3 minutes to give a briefing that was about a project that went on for like 6 months or more. The bloke went home happy. What did I learn? So much more about the German brain!! Germans are known for their emphasis on individualism, masculinity, and uncertainty avoidance. Power distance and long-term orientation are both ranked considerably lower than the others. This illustrates Germany’s belief in equality and opportunity for each citizen, as well as its ability to change and adapt rapidly. Let me put some rapid pointers here.
  • The German thought process is extremely thorough, with each aspect of a project being examined in great detail.Any flaw that you think might go unnoticed, well, you're in for a surprise. This process is obviously often very time-consuming. So the decision-making process in Germany is much slower than in the United States, and this can be troublesome to U.S. executives. Be prepared for the process to take much longer, as there is often a "hidden" group of advisors and decision makers that must approve of any transaction that is to occur. However, once the planning is over, a project will move very quickly and deadlines are expected to be honored.
  • Germans do not like surprises. many a times the Indian mind comes up with great last moment "Eureka Ideas", but Halt! Sudden eleventh hour changes in business transactions, even if they may improve the outcome, are unwelcome. we just spoke about how thorough they like to be.
  • German citizens do not need or expect to be complimented. In Germany, it is usually assumed that everything is satisfactory unless the person hears otherwise. In other words If you don't complain he's done a great job!
  • Punctuality is necessity in Germany. Arrive on time for every appointment, whether for business or social. Being late, even if it is only by a few minutes, is very insulting to a German executive.
  • In business situations, Quick and firm shake of hands at both the beginning and the end of a meeting are a must. Additionally, a handshake may be accompanied with a slight bow or a nod. Reciprocating the nod is a good way to make a good impression, as failure to respond with this nod/bow (especially to a superior) may get you off to a bad start. Be sure to look directly into the person's eyes while shaking hands and also while emphasizing on any point.
  • When being introduced to a woman, wait to see if she extends her hand.
  • Business is viewed as being very serious, and Germans do not appreciate humor in a business context. We usually fall of the track with examples and memoirs, but here they're all a buzz-kill.
  • In business meetings, age takes precedence over youth. If you are in a group setting, the eldest person enters first. (believe me, every time "That" happened!)
  • Germans keep a larger personal space around them, approximately 6 inches more space than North Americans or the Chinese do.( we'll talk about these blokes soon ) However, it is not unusual that when in line at a mall or a store cashier, Germans will crowd up very close to the person in front of them.
  • People that have worked together for years still shake hands each morning as if it were the first time they met.
  • German men frequently greet each other with Herr 'last name', even when they know each other very well. Its Just like saying Mr. Sharma or Mr. XYZ.
  • Germans are able to consume large quantities of beer in one evening, but public drunkenness is not acceptable. It is best to know your limits, especially in Bavaria where two liters of beer is an ordinary evening. However here In India they chug comparatively more than an average Indian liver can tolerate hence its better to pipe down on the beer for at one point of time you will be droopy and the guy who's listening to you won't.
  • Typically, you do not wait to be seated in German restaurants, and it is not uncommon to share a table with strangers.
  • Lastly, DO NOT mention the war or Mister You- Know-Who (ever).
Interestingly, there are so many things that I have learned through the years and I could go on writing. But lets save it for another time! So.. next time you do business with a German, You won't return empty handed! The Waterman
item.excerpt:encoded: On a planet with more than a million cultures, lets take a peep into the customs one of the most masculine lineage of Europe!

item.title: ooh a Problem!! Let's KILL ourselves!
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
Not long ago we had the news channels bustling, trying to provide profound and in depth coverage about this famous actress who had hung herself in her apartment and later the fog cleared only to reveal it was a failed love affair. A few days ago a 70 year old shopkeeper in a small town ended with the same fate because his shop in the local market was demolished for urban developmental purposes, road widening to be precise. No media coverage for you unimportant old timer!
It made me wonder, Why does the human mind sometimes choose to end life so abruptly? There are more than a million microbes that can kill us, poisons, chemicals, war, genetic disorders, accidents and the list of endless ways to un-wantingly perish. But still there are people who race against all these grotesque competitors and not only that, they also make use of their then non working brains to find new ways of killing themselves.
Non working brains? why? well for once if they can think of a way to finish the suffering they can obviously think of a way out! and by a way out i mean to live and solve the problems they themselves created!
Outrageous it might sound to some who cherish life and its sweet nectary fruits. but Studies show people who take a step towards certain death are either the ones that have a smaller social circle or are under tremendous stress and have no room to blow off the steam.
Japan has a jungle that is called the suicide forest of japan, where people actually go to do it. judging by the number of dust laden cars in the tourist parking the government assumes the person to be dead in a few days and impounds the cars, and there have been tens of thousands of suicides in that place that its just spooky to go in on a trek. For you can see abandoned tents, ropes hanging on trees, clothes, personal belongings scattered around the woods. It has a peculiar eeriness that just spooks the crap out of every tourist. Japan arguably also happens to be a country with the most number of suicides on the planet.
It makes you wonder, people who usually opt for this easy way out- according to scientific study - are usually between the age group 14 to 34. When we see the figures and the demographics, there are so many factors that can help us understand why does one choose this pathway.
For starters we humans are no different than ants or honeybees, we have a chain of command, we have sense of superiority, and we also strive to gain a social status. In a nutshell This social animal has a brain that is hard wired to be social, take that out with the help of unnecessary work load and aspirations that are sky high and beyond capacity, this creature called man then forgets everything else and runs behind the more tangible comforts. Skeptics might find this a total no no but its as true as it can be. In the race to achieve something that a person cant even define and vaguely terms it as a career (monetary issues) or loose of something cherished (person or thing that one is usually very attached) or one particular person whom he or she fails to woo is usually the reason for committing suicide.
In all the above cases we see one thing in common. Focused desire. If these people can successfully focus on what they have lost then they can definitely look beyond their losses with the help of other people and fond new reasons to live.
In the U.S of A there was an experiment conducted by psychologists and what they did was, that gave puppies to a group of people who had approached a rehab for trying to commit suicide and within a year they found drastic changes in the behavioral patterns of these individuals. Moral of the story, when man changes the focus from one thing to another, it successfully diverts his mind from a path that he should not have chosen in the first place.
Our brain is a tricky little bugger, keep it occupied and it might work wonders and keep it without purpose and there are a number of ways one can fall into the pit of depression and it goes on building within like a volcano only to erupt in the future.
To even think of finishing ones own life might sound lame to 99% of the population but there is the one guy or girl who goes through tremendous amount of resolution and an immense steadfast belief that one has to do it and not to back off. Imagine a person writing a suicide note, at that point of time this person has made up his/her mind that they wont back off and this stage is what should be avoided. A resolute person who has summed it all up and is about to jump off is still in a dilemma of weather to back off or not. that itself tells us the human soul still wants to live. and we forcefully make a haphazard decision based on naive short-sightedness. It is as idiotic as slamming into a glass door, DOOR! doors can open! solutions can be found! all you have to do is find a way out.
So how can we deal with this? As responsible human beings. What each and every one of us can be a bit more vigilant towards people around us. And keep an eye on people who might need emotional assistance or an agony aunt. people might not readily talk and open up but with a careful approach they might give in and let off some steam.
As normal people everyone needs to be more social involve as many people as you can in your daily lives, smile more, and talk more because you never know who might find your smile a reason to look forward to hope. And to those special ones among us with no hopes, Indulge yourselves in a hobby, yes any hobby, get into any lamest and wackiest hobby you can think of. It can be music to gardening or even getting a pet. Just make sure its a legal pet and not a pet that can strangle you or eat you for breakfast. Being alive doesn't only mean to breathe, eat twice and poop! Being alive means so much more, being alive is like walking back home from school.
Problems are like speed bumps and you are a car, go figure how many times you thought of throwing away your car into a valley just because you it a speed breaker on the road?
Point is the road never ends.
The Waterman

wp:comment.wp:comment_content: The topic of ending up own life is one of the top most research subject s of psychologists. It is a strong negative wave in humans mind which leads to stop the breath. The ultimate solution can be we respect each human beings and if we cant make anyone happy atleast should not make any one sad.
very well said!

item.title: Travelling on an invisible Road
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
There was this random thought that I had the other day, I don't remember when exactly but I guess I was watching a documentary on T.V., when the thought hit me like a battering ram, as people, as humans where are we headed? With a feeble life span of six to seven decades we humans dream of things as if we were gonna live for a billion years, hell our planet wont be living for that long.
Everyday begins with aspirations, various sorts of tensions and man made problems that we all care about. But never has man stopped in his tracks to think about something that actually left me stumped.
Where are we headed? Do we have any control on our future ? As individuals ? As a species? I guess not. To be precise we never die, I'm not being theological or a religious fanatic. My statement here is based on scientific logic. Technically we pass on our genes, our DNA to forthcoming generations. Not only us but every species thrives to spread their genes as vastly as they can and propagate into nature to sustain their particular species in this large biosphere. This simply is the law of nature. Hence speaking we never die we only transform from one to another.
But to think of human beings to be evolved from Neanderthals who in turn evolved from lesser apes all over the world, we see a pattern, a very simple pattern, man adopted to his lifestyle and evolved accordingly, the apes were in need of continuous food supply so they habituated seashores and riverbanks, continuous interaction with water gave their forthcoming species certain changes in physical appearance, like webbed fingers. I am not backing up the aquatic ape theory (wiki for more info) as I personally feel it is far fetched. Then the Neanderthals for some reason became more erect made complicated tools and the homo-sapiens that came later made sure their species a much efficient and resourceful species prevailed.
Now that our species has already undergone so many phenomenal changes from the first homo sapiens, this never-ending adaptation process will go on as much as it can. One cannot say weather the biological complexity will increase or decrease here because adaptation involves inclusion of external factors like environment, food, resistance to diseases etc etc.
We are today not susceptible to certain diseases that are forefathers were, similarly our future generations will be more immune, on the other hand technological advances are headed today towards ease of life, man has automated everything that not long ago was a back breaking toil for people. Thus on the other hand human today are less physically fit than our predecessors. I keep hearing to stories of longevity and strength from history and today this story has changed. Eating habits have changed, nutritional value of food has changed, there are myriad factors that are currently effecting us.
As the old native American saying goes the Axe forgets but the tree remembers, similarly we as an individual never give importance to these things but our body remembers, it makes minuscule changes and alterations in the genes and the forthcoming off-springs are born with those changes, just like Darvin explained the long necks of giraffes. Now in this case what those changes could be in humans no one can say but we can pin point the aspects that might lead to these changes.
In a computerized world where each and everyone of you reading this is more proficient in computers than your parents, you shall inadvertently give birth to kids who are obviously more proficient than you! The future humans have to be hence technologically sound, unless a global step is taken towards physical fitness, the future generations will only have big brains and nothing else.
There is a theory that says our sun shall bulge into a red dwarf in a billion years and expand so much that the first two planets will be engulfed and earth which currently lies in the "Goldilocks zone" shall only remain as a dead ball of fire. Some day, long before this happens the future humans might even leave this planet. They might reach and colonize other planets for in just a few thousand years of coming into being we have developed tools to reach mars, imagine technology taking a leap only a million years into the future, why should we deny the possibility of such phenomenal feats? Unless there is a meteorite that comes gift wrapped and wipes us out like our beloved dinosaurs, we are bound to develop and evolve progressively.
The mere understanding of the universe is something I think is the most responsible factor for our development. other species on this planet cannot do that. The most intelligent species after humans are the orcas and dolphins but their primary motive in life is to propagate and eat thats all! So they evolved into apex predators and shall continue to do so. Similarly human motive is to gain more knowledge. We do not live our lives only to have kids and feed them and die. We live to achieve, dream, aspire! hence we are writing that in our genes generation by generation. Inadvertently teaching our future generations to be more intelligent. Our bodies on the other hand are adapting to these new factors. We have found ways to manipulate our own genes the very blueprint of our species and are now designing babies. The day is not far when we evolve into a more efficient and stable species.
But that again is just a possibility, numerous factors must be taken into consideration.
Sometimes I sit back and wonder. All those UFO sightings and horseshit conspiracies we see around us and the ancient Incan drawings of astronauts in their pyramids. Who knows if they are just time traveling humans of the future.
The Waterman
item.excerpt:encoded: The wood is our past and the smoke our future and today the fire burns. - ancient eskimo saying.

item.title: Come bow before us!
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
Today in India a terrorist was hanged. For he had killed some 257 people way back in '93. And somehow all of the paid-media has fallen into a frenzy. Debating weather he should have lived. And hence questioning the government that is trying hard to root out the decay from a country that is building up slowly along with its endemic parasites.
On a bigger picture. As moronic as the news channels can be. Instead of looking towards a brighter future of a country, they prefer bringing people in Spotlight. People who with the help of their socio-contradictory concepts make sure they remain in that spotlight.
Media is nothing but a marketing tool. Apart from the advertising (facet of the day long airing schedule), it comes to my notice that only a little time is given to quality information. It helps keep people glued to their respective screens.
All day long we have reporters and so called subject matter experts sitting in split screens debating about a certain event and my question is directly to the viewers of these idiotic programs. Do you or do you not think that you are capable of coming up with your own opinions about things happening around you?
As I write this article there is an ongoing discussion about the undetonated bombs lying around in the middle East during the U.S invasions. And the discussion then suddenly drifts towards the Chinese economy?
There are billions of people of our own species that have their own set of problems. And yet go unnoticed. This fact is so disturbing that when I come to think of it, of all the times that I have made myself sit in front of my TV to see the news I have never seen a reporter airing from a sub-saharan terrain showing the current condition of people and children perishing due to lack of resources. I have never seen a report on the child trafficking done all over the globe. I have never seen a report on the reality! All I see is celebrities tweeting something that gives the news channels a gum to chew on for the whole unproductive day.
And it is not the mass media that can be pointed out to be the culprit. The poor money mongering corporate snakes only air things that appeal to the masses. I mean who wants to see a mother crying for her kids dying in a blast when you can host a debate over why a certain celebrity has gone under the scalpel to get new breast implants?
I am not shunning the Media entirely. There are a handful channels worldwide that are doing a phenomenal job keeping it precise and concise. The rest however is hogwash. Tailored news, Politically biased reviews, Paid media, pointless debates, all of this only makes your mind numb.
To come to think of it; How can the discussion of a handful of people help the masses? Instead of trying to implant thoughts in the minds of the masses the media should rather concentrate on providing better quality material. Real time, non opinionated, unbiased news.
It's so funny that the human mind is so vulnerable to information intake. It believes anything that is told with confidence and a little truth. Truth.. The versions of which however... Is another long story.
The waterman

item.title: Change is not an option
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded: Two meals a day? Insufficient! Three? Adequate! What about one? Are you nuts!?? The human mind is like a colorful clay dough you can make it, shape it, bend it and make it believe whatever you want it to believe. If you believe four meals are inadequate, you will still be hungry. Now tell yourself you shall only eat once in a day for there are millions of people around the globe that go to bed with an empty stomach. This is just another joke. Because believe me no one would willingly want to do that. Point is, that food is a basic need and basic needs are always unevenly spread across the masses. May it be money, shelter, clothing or whatever it is that you think is relevant. And the reason these things are unevenly spread is because resources are unevenly spread. The mighty have the power to pool resources and the weak have to forage whatever scrapes drift their way. Lets go through some scenarios: 1. Let's consider walking through the market. The fruit vendors isolate the bigger better looking fruits and put a bigger price tag and the remaining are cheaper. You choose the costly ones obviously for added quality and value for money. You buy a kilo and head home. Then a time comes when you stash of in the refrigerator along with other stuff. And eventually those fruits come out when there's new stuff growing on it. And go straight into the bin. 2. It's a busy day in the kitchen! Hustle time as all the guests will be arriving any time now. And like any other good and presentable host you have a long three course meal spread out on the table. Say there's a starters, main courses, and desserts that you are an expert at making and you offer them generously. A few hours later all those half eaten cuisines head where? Exactly where you think! Into the bin. 3. You have to take a break out of your routine and what better way to get out and head out to a posh restaurant for quenching the  thirst of your tongue! As you are bored with the food that you eat at home everyday. And change is always good! So you order, wait, eat, pay, and leave. Story doesn't end there! There is lot happening backstage and what you ate was roughly 30-40% of the food used to prepare your dish. And as usual the rest ..... Into... The bin! To look at a bigger picture, you are among the mighty and resource rich masses. And the foragers have no idea how lavishly you brush aside the stale scraps that they could eat with a happy heart and a jaunty stomach! When you do have a few minutes you can boldly click this link to see the big picture. http://www.ffl.org/emergency-relief/world-hunger/ And to those who want to make a difference join Inspire (click link) Spread the world and be the change. People Don't Change When You Give Them an Option ! They Change When They Realise That There's No Other Option !! The rest however will evolve.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Solution to World Hunger

item.title: Meeting a Dragon and Kung fu!
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded:
A while ago we dug down deep with "meeting a beer and sausage" on how to deal with Germans when it comes to making business, which got immense response and praise, also, I received many requests on getting into other business cultures around the world. In this article I would try and get inside a Chinese brain. Reason being, this communist powerhouse plays a huge role in global economy and is Asia's largest industrial super power next only to japan and India.

When we saw the Germans we saw a neatly placed social structure that has made the Germans a fairly conservative lot. However the Chinese are more open and social when compared to the Europeans.

So lets get down to business.

The Chinese mindset basically runs on the concept of Confucianism

Confucianism revolves around the concept of harmonious relationships. If proper behavior through duty, respect and loyalty are shown in the relationships between a boss- employee, family members and friends, society as a whole will function smoothly. When doing business in China it is possible to see how Confucianism affects business practices. Of the less subtle manifestations are an aversion to conflict, maintenance of proper behavior and the preservation of 'face'. Now this 'face' here is tricky business. Roughly translated as 'good reputation', 'respect' or 'honor,' you gotta learn the delicateness of the concept and understand the possible impact it could have on your business in China. (for more extensive details Google 'Gei MianZi') It is critical that you give face, save face and show face when doing business in China.

Lets head to some pointers now.

  • Seniority is very important to the Chinese especially if you are dealing with a State owned or government body. Instead of addressing the other party as Mr or Mrs so and so, it is always appropriate to address the other party by his designation i.e., Chairman So and So, Director So and So or Manager So and So.
  • Meetings must be scheduled in advance.
  • Some literature regarding your company should be forwarded to introduce the company.
  • Try and book meetings between April - June and September - October.
  • Avoid all national holidays especially Chinese New Year.
  • Prior to any meeting always send an agenda. The Chinese approach meetings differently than the germans, so rather than beginning with minor or side issues and working your way up to the core issue, reverse this.
  • In China, meetings start with the shaking of hands (one or both clasped) and a slight nod of the head. Be sure not to be overly vigorous when shaking hands as the Chinese will interpret this as aggressive.
  • The Chinese are not keen on physical contact - especially when doing business. Be sure not to slap, pat or put your arm around someone's shoulders.
  • Punctuality is vital when doing business in China. Ensure you are early as late arrivals are seen as an insult. Meetings should begin with some brief small talk.
  • Body language and movement are both areas you should be conscious of when doing business in China. You should always be calm, poised and controlled. Body posture should always be formal and attentive as this shows you have self-control and are worthy of respect.
  • If this is your first meeting then talk of your experiences in China so far (who doesn't like flattery?). But keep it positive and avoid anything political.
  • Never become too informal and avoid humor. This is not because the Chinese are humorless but jokes may be lost in translation and hence be redundant.
  • The Chinese are renowned for being tough negotiators. Their primary aim in negotiations is 'concessions and rebates'. Always bear this in mind when formulating your own strategy. You must be willing to show compromise and ensure their negotiators feel they have gained major concessions. One known strategy for Chinese negotiators is to begin negotiations showing humility and deference. This is designed to present themselves as vulnerable and weak. You, the stronger, will be expected to help them through concessions.
  • Business cards are exchanged on an initial meeting. Make sure one side of the card has been translated and try and print the Chinese letters using gold ink as this is an auspicious color. Mention your company, rank and any qualifications you hold. When receiving a card place it in a case rather than in a wallet or pocket.
  • Make sure you have done your homework before doing business in China. The Chinese plan extensively and will know your business and possibly you inside out.
  • Lastly, be patient and never show anger or frustration. Practice your best 'poker face' before negotiating with the Chinese. Once they figure out that you are uncomfortable they will exploit the weakness.
  • Decisions will take a long time either because there is a lack of urgency or simultaneous negotiations are taking place with competitors or because the decision makers are not confident enough.
Coming down to other business interactions: The Chinese are big drinkers especially in Northern and Western China. It does not matter if it is lunch or dinner; as long as a meal is being hosted, there will be alcohol.

Chinese wine is the favorite, followed by red wine and beer. Chinese wine is more like fuel than liquor, having a alcohol concentration as high as 60%! No matter how good a drinker you may think of yourself, never, ever challenge a Chinese into a drinking contest. They will win, hands down, thank you beijing, Good night! It is often seen as rude not to drink with the Chinese in a formal dinner. To maintain your sanity, either claim to be a non alcoholic or plead medical grounds as an excuse. This will let you off the hook with little or minimal drinks. Better yet, bring a partner who can drink on your behalf!

A quick pointer for Indians and westerners: It is impolite to fight for the bill or worst, split the bills.

And lastly there are some taboo areas in social conversations with the Chinese. Try to avoid these conversational topics as much as possible. I have seen many nasty arguments as a result of these topics:

1. You must neither mention that Taiwan is an independent state or a country nor point out that Arunachal Pradesh is an Indian state. 2. You must NEVER praise the Japanese or be seen to be good buddies with them. 3. During casual business meets do keep in mind that you can condemn Mao Tse Tung (communist revolutionary that people hate) but avoid criticizing Deng Hsiao Ping ( communist revolutionary that people love). 4. You must not praise Shanghai in front of natives of Beijing and similarly vice versa.

Other than that, you are pretty safe to converse with the Chinese anything under the sun!

Until next time Yiche shunli!!

The Waterman
item.excerpt:encoded: China's business culture and etiquette is very much different from Western business practice.It is good advice if you can learn some basic Chinese business culture before making a business trip.

item.title: The "like" button of life
item.dc:creator: iamwaterman
item.content:encoded: A while ago I was out driving through the city. I was in the mood so I just turned up the music put on some Rock and roll from the 80's and I was in my own world of bliss. My version of letting off the steam. It began raining soon and it all got even more nostalgic. The music, the cool fragrant air in my car, the rain, the greenery around me. As I took a turn on my way back I saw a man. Must've been in his 50's. Had a rag of a coat, some tethered pants, grizzly Grey beard and shabby hair. He had eyes that spoke loudly, they had much melancholy and longing, it was not more than a few seconds that I had a glimpse of this guy. But so much had been spoken already, I could clearly see he had had a good life and somehow on his way down the road he got hit by destiny and his world must've fallen apart. He had this fairly large photo frame in his hand and was crossing the road while looking at it. actually thats how he came across me. Out of curiosity I lowered my window and tried get a look at the frame and from my distance I could make out a family picture. Someone sitting down and people standing around that person. The air in my car wasn't cool any more, nor the music loud. I do not know weather it was my pondering on my thoughts or I had absent minded-ly turned everything down. I was just driving blankly all the way back home. everything was quiet but inside my head there was a big maelstrom of thoughts. And then it struck me. As hard as a bulldozer. What I have now, all my amenities, no matter how hard I must've worked for, to get them, are all so delicately superficial. Nothing lasts forever, and good times- definitely not. Every time we got out to have a good time we spew out money as everyone else and try to savor life's greatest pleasures. I think thats the right thing to do. To do it when you can. Who knows what would life give you or take away from you.
